Acronyms

+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| **Acronym** | **Definition**                                                                                          |
+:============+:========================================================================================================+
| 6S          | Second Simulation of the Satellite Signal in the Solar Spectrum                                         |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| AERONET     | Aerosol Robotic Network                                                                                 |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| AC          | Atmospheric Correction                                                                                  |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| ACIX2       | Atmospheric Corrections Intercomparison eXercise II                                                     |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| AMIP        | MERRA-2 Atmospheric Model Intercomparison Project                                                       |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| AOT         | Aerosol Optical Thickness                                                                               |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| ATBD        | Algorithm Theoretical Basis Document                                                                    |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| BC          | Black Carbon aerosols                                                                                   |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| GPL         | GNU Public Licence                                                                                      |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| GPU         | Graphics Processing Unit                                                                                |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| HDF4        | Hierarchical Data Format4                                                                               |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| MERRA-2     | Modern-Era Retrospective analysis for Research and Applications, Version 2                              |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| MIR         | Medium InfraRed                                                                                         |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| NetCDF      | Network Common Data Form                                                                                |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| NIR         | Near InfraRed                                                                                           |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| NRT         | Near Real Time                                                                                          |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| OC          | Organic Carbon aerosols                                                                                 |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| PUGS        | Product User Guide Specification                                                                        |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| PUM         | Product User Manual                                                                                     |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| PQAR        | Product Quality Assessment Report                                                                       |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| RSRF        | Relative Spectral Response Function                                                                     |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| RSS         | Residual Sum of Square                                                                                  |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| SA          | Surface Albedo                                                                                          |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| SAA         | Sun Azimuth Angle                                                                                       |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| SMAC        | Simplified Model for Atmospheric Corrections                                                            |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| SMAC-CL     | SMAC model implemented with OPEN-CL language                                                            |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| SPOT        | Satellite Probatoire d'Observation de la Terre                                                          |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| SRF         | Spectral Response Function                                                                              |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| SS          | Sea Salt aerosols                                                                                       |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| SU          | SUlphate aerosols                                                                                       |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| SWIR        | Short-Wave InfraRed                                                                                     |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| SZA         | Sun Zenith Angle                                                                                        |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| TOA         | Top Of the Atmosphere                                                                                   |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| TOC         | Top Of Canopy                                                                                           |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| TOC-r       | Top Of Canopy Reflectance                                                                               |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| UTC         | Universal Time Coordinates                                                                              |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| VAA         | View Azimuth Angle                                                                                      |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| VEGETATION  | The medium resolution sensor onboard SPOT4 and SPOT5                                                    |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| VGT         | VEGETATION sensor                                                                                       |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| VITO        | Vlaamse Instelling voor Technologisch Onderzoek (Flemish Institute for Technological Research), Belgium |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| VR          | Validation Report                                                                                       |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| VZA         | View Zenith Angle                                                                                       |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| WGS         | World Geodetic System                                                                                   |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| WSA         | White-Sky Albedo                                                                                        |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

Scope of the document

This Algorithm Theoretical Basis Document (ATBD) describes the atmospheric correction (AC) processing chain for deriving Top-of-Canopy (TOC) directional reflectances from Top-of-Atmosphere (TOA) reflectances measured by the Proba-V/VGT sensors. The processing implements rigorous uncertainty propagation following the FIDUCEO (Fidelity and Uncertainty in Climate Data Records from Earth Observations) methodology.

# Introduction

The processing follows a hierarchical structure:

**Level 1C** → **Level 2A** (atmospherically corrected pixels)

```         
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    INPUT DATA                           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  • TOA reflectances (B1, B2, B3, B4) + uncertainties    │
│  • Geometry (SZA, VZA, SAA, VAA)                        │
│  • Geolocation (lat, lon)                               │
│  • Status map (cloud mask)                              │
└────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                 │
                 ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              AUXILIARY DATA RETRIEVAL                   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  • DEM → altitude z, uncertainty Δz                     │
│  • MERRA-2 → P₀, T, UO₃, UH₂O, τa⁵⁵⁰, aerosol species   │
│  • AMIP ensemble → aerosol model uncertainty            │
└────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                 │
                 ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│         ATMOSPHERIC CORRECTION (SMAC-CL)                │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Process 1: Direct atmospheric correction               │
│  Process 2: Uncertainty propagation                     │
│    - Analytical Jacobians (Rtoa, UO₃, UH₂O)             │
│    - Finite difference Jacobians (Ps, τa⁵⁵⁰)            │
│    - Ensemble uncertainty (aerosol model)               │
│  Process 3: Radiative transfer approximation errors     │
└────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                 │
                 ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    OUTPUT DATA                          │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  • TOC reflectances (B1, B2, B3, B4)                    │
│  • Total uncertainty ΔRtoc                              │
│  • Optionally, Uncertainty components by source         │
│  • Quality flags                                        │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## What's New in v1.1

This v1.1 revision keeps the full v1.0 content below **unchanged** and adds the following new material:

- **Appendix D: Implementation Notes** — performance optimizations (ancillary Zarr cache, tile-based processing, local output file, single NetCDF handle, DEM cropping, copy-on-write shared state), the float32 overflow fix for `SLP`/`T10M`, and known limitations (adjacency effects, Köppen-Geiger regionalization).
- **Appendix E: SMACCL OpenCL Kernel API** — the Python host wrapper API (`Smaccl.run`), the OpenCL kernel signature (`devicecl.cl`), the output variable mapping, and the `ISmaccl` product-level wrapper API, as implemented in the HYGEOS `smaccl` repository.
- A **critical bug-fix note** on surface pressure computation: `SLP` and `T10M` must be stored as float32 (not float16) in the ancillary cache to avoid overflow in `log(R·T)` (see Appendix D.2 and the note under Effect 4: Surface Pressure Uncertainty).
- Confirmation that **DEM interpolation** uses linear interpolation (an improvement over the nearest-neighbor method originally specified), and that **adjacency effects** and **Köppen-Geiger regionalization** are, respectively, a known limitation and a planned feature.

# Input and Auxiliary Data

## Level 1C Input Data

The data used as inputs are Proba-V-VGT1-VGT2 Top-Of-Atmosphere reflectances in 4 spectral bands (Table 5) and associated geometrical information and basic classification flags. Let us note that the viewing geometry could be different between VIS-NIR and SWIR bands. The RSRF's of each band is slightly different between the 3 cameras labelled LEFT, CENTER and RIGHT that cover Proba-V field of view but one RSRF's for VGTs. The philosophy of the AC algorithm is to process the data band per band without the use of any spectral, spatial or temporal features. Therefore, the format of the input data is not important and the processing of individual pixels for selected bands or an entire Level 1 tile is similar.

**Spectral Bands** (Proba-V example):

+----------+-------------------------+-----------+-----------+---------------------------------------------+
| Band     | Central wavelength (nm) | FWHM (nm) | Corrected | Absorbing gas/ transmission\*               |
+:=========+:========================+:==========+:==========+:============================================+
| B1       | 463.5                   | 47.0      | yes       | O~3~ (0.994)                                |
+----------+-------------------------+-----------+-----------+---------------------------------------------+
| B2       | 655.0                   | 80.0      | yes       | O~3~ (0.976); H~2~O (0.989); O~2~ (0.981)   |
+----------+-------------------------+-----------+-----------+---------------------------------------------+
| B3       | 839.0                   | 130.0     | yes       | O~3~ (0.999); H~2~O (0.893); O~2~ (0.999)   |
+----------+-------------------------+-----------+-----------+---------------------------------------------+
| B4       | 1602.5                  | 65.0      | yes       | CO~2~ (0.969); H~2~O (0.995); CH~4~ (0.998) |
+----------+-------------------------+-----------+-----------+---------------------------------------------+

Table 1: VGT's spectral bands and potential atmospheric gaseous absorbers

**Required Variables per Pixel:**

- TOA-r(λ): Top-of-atmosphere reflectance for each band
- DTOA-r(λ): TOA reflectance uncertainty
- Viewing and solar azimuth angles (VAA, VAA_SWIR and SAA) \[0, 360°\]
- Viewing and solar zenith angles (VZA, VZA_SWIR and SZA) \[0, 90°\].
- Geolocation: latitude, longitude
- Status Map: Cloud mask flags

## Digital Elevation Model (DEM)

The GTOPO30 (Global 30 Arc-Second Elevation) dataset is used for assigning a surface elevation to each pixel. It is downloaded as a set of binary files (<https://lta.cr.usgs.gov/GTOPO30>) and then re-assembled as a unique [NetCDF]{.underline} file.

- Frequency: Static

- Spatial Grid: 2D, full extent of latitude from 90 degrees south to 90 degrees north, and the full extent of longitude from 180 degrees west to 180 degrees east. The horizontal grid spacing is 30-arc seconds.

- Dimensions of 21,600 rows and 43,200 columns. The horizontal coordinate system is decimal degrees of latitude and longitude referenced to WGS84.

- The vertical units represent elevation above mean sea level (elev) and are expressed in meters. The values range from -407 to 8,752. It is complemented by the spatial standard deviation of the altitude (Delev in meters) computed in 3x3 pixel boxes.

**Specifications:**

- Spatial resolution: 30 arc-seconds (\~1 km)
- Coverage: Global (-90° to +90° lat, -180° to +180° lon)
- Dimensions: 21,600 rows × 43,200 columns
- Vertical range: -407 to +8,752 m above mean sea level
- Additional field: **Delev** - standard deviation of altitude computed in 3×3 pixel boxes (proxy for geolocation uncertainty impact)

**Usage:**

```         
z = elev(lat, lon)           # Surface elevation
Δz = Delev(lat, lon)         # Elevation uncertainty
```

> **Implementation Note (v1.1):** the implementation uses **linear interpolation** (via `scipy.interpolate.RegularGridInterpolator`) to sample `elev`/`Delev` at the satellite grid, rather than nearest-neighbor. This is a reasonable improvement, giving smoother elevation transitions at tile boundaries; the impact is negligible given the 90 m GTOPO30 resolution versus \~1 km VGT pixels. The global DEM is also cropped to the product's lat/lon extent before loading (\~1.9 GB → \~0.2 GB), see Appendix D.1.

## MERRA-2 Reanalysis Products

The Modern-Era Retrospective analysis for Research and Applications version 2 (MERRA-2) is a NASA atmospheric reanalysis for the satellite era using the Goddard Earth Observing System Model, Version 5 (GEOS-5) with its Atmospheric Data Assimilation System (ADAS), version 5.12.4. It is the data source for all atmospheric parameters (<https://gmao.gsfc.nasa.gov/reanalysis/MERRA-2/>). The README file for the [NetCDF]{.underline} MERRA-2 products is available here:

(<https://goldsmr4.gesdisc.eosdis.nasa.gov/data/MERRA2/M2I1NXINT.5.12.4/doc/MERRA2.README.pdf>), while the full product specification is available here:

(<https://gmao.gsfc.nasa.gov/pubs/docs/Bosilovich785.pdf>).

The datasets are available from 1^st^ January 1980 until present. They can be downloaded from several sites, for example ([https://disc.sci.gsfc.nasa.gov/daac-bin/FTPSubset2.pl](https://www.google.com/url?q=https%3A%2F%2Fdisc.sci.gsfc.nasa.gov%2Fdaac-bin%2FFTPSubset2.pl&sa=D&sntz=1&usg=AFQjCNHxhCMbiw1OeM3uf72kiq0W9oLYrg)).

Two products are currently used for all atmospheric parameters:

- tavg1_2d_slv_Nx: 2-dimensional, 1-Hourly, Time-Averaged for physical parameters, ozone and water vapour.

- tavg1_2d_aer_Nx: 2-dimensional, 1-Hourly, Time-Averaged for aerosol diagnostics.

**Overview:**

- Temporal coverage: 1980-present
- Temporal resolution: 1-hourly, from 00:30 UTC (time average)
- Spatial grid: 0.5° × 0.625°
  - 361 lat (from -90 to 90)
  - 576 lon (from -180 to 179.375)

**Retrieved Parameters:**

+--------------+------------+-------------------------------------+---------------+
| Variable     | MERRA-2 ID | Description                         | Units         |
+:=============+:===========+:====================================+:==============+
| UO₃          | TO3        | Total column ozone                  | Dobson units  |
+--------------+------------+-------------------------------------+---------------+
| UH₂O         | TQV        | Total precipitable water vapor      | kg·m⁻²        |
+--------------+------------+-------------------------------------+---------------+
| P₀           | SLP        | Sea-level pressure                  | Pa            |
+--------------+------------+-------------------------------------+---------------+
| T            | T10M       | Temperature at 10m                  | K             |
+--------------+------------+-------------------------------------+---------------+
| τa⁵⁵⁰(total) | TOTEXTTAU  | Total aerosol extinction AOD\@550nm | dimensionless |
+--------------+------------+-------------------------------------+---------------+
| τa⁵⁵⁰(BC)    | BCEXTTAU   | Black carbon AOD\@550nm             | dimensionless |
+--------------+------------+-------------------------------------+---------------+
| τa⁵⁵⁰(OC)    | OCEXTTAU   | Organic carbon AOD\@550nm           | dimensionless |
+--------------+------------+-------------------------------------+---------------+
| τa⁵⁵⁰(DU)    | DUEXTTAU   | Dust AOD\@550nm                     | dimensionless |
+--------------+------------+-------------------------------------+---------------+
| τa⁵⁵⁰(SU)    | SUEXTTAU   | Sulfate AOD\@550nm                  | dimensionless |
+--------------+------------+-------------------------------------+---------------+
| τa⁵⁵⁰(SS)    | SSEXTTAU   | Sea salt AOD\@550nm                 | dimensionless |
+--------------+------------+-------------------------------------+---------------+

**Interpolation Method:**

- Spatial: Bilinear interpolation to pixel location
- Temporal: Linear interpolation between hourly time steps

## MERRA-2 AMIP Ensemble for Aerosol Uncertainty

The purpose of this dataset is to estimate aerosol model uncertainty through ensemble spread

The MERRA-2 AMIP (Atmospheric Model Intercomparison Project) dataset consists of a 10-member ensemble of free-running simulations with the GEOS atmospheric model (<https://gmao.gsfc.nasa.gov/gmao-products/merra-2/merra-2-amip_merra-2/>) . The model version, along with imposed boundary conditions, is configured identically to the GEOS model used in the MERRA-2 reanalysis dataset. It covers the years from 1980 to 2017.

The dataset can be accessed here (<https://portal.nccs.nasa.gov/datashare/gmao_m2amip/>). It is organized in 10 directories, one for each run of the ensemble (example m2amip01), then one directory for each year and then one directory per month. This monthly average dataset is used for estimating the spread of the aerosol optical thickness between models and is supposed to be a proxy for the uncertainty on the MERRA-2 AOT's. The spatial grid is the same as MERRA2 daily data described in section 2.3.

The file we are using is tavgM_2d_aer_Nx.YYYYMM.nc4 with the same parameters as in section 2.3

[Note]{.underline} : For Proba-V data in 2018 to 2020, it is expected to use AMIP data of 2017 for the corresponding month.

**Description:**

- 10-member ensemble of GEOS atmospheric model free-running simulations
- Same model version and boundary conditions as MERRA-2 reanalysis
- Period: 1980-2017 (for 2018-2020 data, use 2017 corresponding month)
- Product: `tavgM_2d_aer_Nx` (monthly averages)
- Variables: Same aerosol species as MERRA-2 (TOTEXTTAU, DUEXTTAU, etc.)

**Implementation:**

``` For each ensemble member j (j=``1..``10): ```\
``` ``τa,AMIPj``⁵⁵⁰(species) = spatially interpolate monthly mean ```

## SMAC Coefficients Database

**Description**: Pre-computed coefficients for 148 aerosol models covering realistic atmospheric mixtures (see Section 8)

**Coverage:**

- Sensors: VGT1, VGT2, Proba-V (LEFT, CENTER, RIGHT cameras)
- Aerosol models: 148 mixtures of 5 basic components (DU, SU, OC, BC, SS)
- Relative humidity: Fixed at 80%

**Content per aerosol model:**

- Gaseous absorption coefficients (O₃, H₂O, CO₂, CH₄)
- Aerosol scattering coefficients
- Look-up tables for atmospheric functions

## C3S Surface Albedo product (experimental)

This product gives a background BRDF shape to estimate the surface BRDF effects on atmospheric correction. The dataset is available on VITO infrastructure and is described here (<https://dast.copernicus-climate.eu/documents/satellite-albedo/D1.3.4-v2.0_ATBD_CDR_SA_MULTI_SENSOR_v2.0_PRODUCTS_v1.1.pdf>).

**Description**:

- Ten days Surface BRDF kernel model parameters (k~0~,k~1~,k~2~) from C3S Surface Albedo project (see section 8.5 for a mathematical description).

**Overview :**

- Temporal coverage: VGT 1998-2014

- Temporal resolution: 10 days

- Spatial resolution: 1km

- Spatial grid: 10°x10° tiles

**Implementation** :

Computing and spatially interpolate from them two normalized parameters $k_{1}^{p}\ and\ k_{2}^{p}$.

$$k_{1}^{p} = \ \frac{k_{1}}{k_{0}}\ ;\ k_{2}^{p} = \ \frac{k_{2}}{k_{0}}$$

# Atmospheric corrections algorithm

## SMAC Algorithm Overview

Pixels are considered for atmospheric correction if they are not marked as "cloud-covered" or "contaminated" in the cloud mask of input data. TOC directional reflectance estimates are obtained by applying SMAC (Rahman and Dedieu, 1994), a Simplified Method for the Atmospheric Correction of satellite measurements in the solar spectrum to the TOA reflectances.

The choice of the SMAC algorithm is supported by the following arguments:

- It is **operational** and largely used in the land community and already implemented in the Copernicus Global Land Service and Proba-V processing lines.

- It is a **robust and generic** algorithm; thus, it minimizes the dependence on the sensor which is a good thing when one wants to build a multi sensor long time series with limited biases.

- The correction algorithm is based on the 6S radiative transfer formulation of the satellite signal (Vermote et al., 1997) and is **analytical** It is thus adapted to an error propagation analysis.

**In our implementation, all the pertinent radiative quantities are parameterized as a function of auxiliary gridded data** as described in the section 2:

- Gas content (mainly ozone and water vapor for the spectral channels considered in this project)

- Aerosol content and aerosol type

- Molecular scattering mainly driven by the sea-level surface pressure and the surface elevation.

This approach is justified by increasing **accuracy** of the Reanalysis datasets like MERRA-2 or CAMS, which are **not sensors biased, without spatial, temporal or spectral gaps**. It is particularly well adapted to the reprocessing of Fundamental Climate Data Records.

## Equations

All the parametrization is contained in the SMAC coefficients.

+-------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------+
|       | $$T = T_{g}\left( \theta_{s},\theta_{v} \right)T_{sca}\left( \theta_{s} \right)T_{sca}\left( \theta_{v} \right)$$ | \(1\) |
+=======+:==================================================================================================================+:======+
|       | $$R = R_{toa} - R_{atm}\left( \theta_{s},\theta_{v},\Delta\Phi \right)T_{g}\left( \theta_{s},\theta_{v} \right)$$ | \(2\) |
+-------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------+
|       | $$R_{toc}\left( \theta_{s},\theta_{v},\Delta\Phi \right) = \frac{R}{T + s_{atm}R}$$                               | \(3\) |
+-------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------+

Where:

\- **T**: Total atmospheric transmission

\- **T~g~**: Gaseous transmission (O₃, H₂O, CO₂, CH₄)

\- **T~sca~**: Scattering transmission (Rayleigh + aerosol)

\- **R**: Intermediate corrected reflectance

\- **R~atm~**: Atmospheric path radiance

\- **s~atm~**: Atmospheric spherical albedo

## Sensitivities

It is possible to derive the atmospherically corrected TOC directional reflectance uncertainty $\Delta R_{toc}$ from the **uncertainties on the TOA reflectance**, on the **atmospheric reflectances and transmittances**, which are themselves derived from the **uncertainties** **on the** **gaseous content**, **surface pressure** **aerosol load and model**. The sensitivities (Jacobians) to the inputs are obtained in the following manner

### Sensitivity to TOA reflectance

The radiometric uncertainty of the TOA reflectance is$\ \mathrm{\Delta}R_{toa}$. The resulting uncertainty about the TOC directional reflectance$\ \mathrm{\Delta}_{R_{toc}}^{R_{toa}}$, is obtained:

+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                                                                               |
+:=============================================================================================================================:+
| $$\mathrm{\Delta}_{R_{toc}}^{R_{toa}} = \left| \frac{\partial R_{toc}}{\partial R_{toa}}.\mathrm{\Delta}R_{toa} \right|$$ (4) |
+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

------------------------------------------------------------------------

where $\frac{\partial R_{toc}}{\partial R_{toa}}$ is the sensitivity of TOC directional reflectance to any change in TOA reflectance. It is the Jacobian$\ J_{R_{toc}}^{R_{toa}}$.

Within SMAC, it can be derived analytically:

+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                                                          |
+:========================================================================================================:+
| $$J_{R_{toc}}^{R_{toa}} = \ \frac{\partial R_{toc}}{\partial R}\frac{\partial R}{\partial R_{toa}}$$ (5) |
+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

------------------------------------------------------------------------

after remarking that$\ \frac{\partial R}{\partial R_{toa}} = 1,$ and using Eqs. 2 and 3

+----------+------------------------------------------+----------+
|          | $$\eta = \frac{1}{({T + s}_{atm}\ R)}$$  | \(6\)    |
+==========+:=========================================+:=========+
|          | $$J_{R_{toc}}^{R_{toa}} = \eta^{2}\ T$$  | \(7\)    |
+----------+------------------------------------------+----------+

### Sensitivity to Ozone and Water Vapor

The sensitivity of TOC directional reflectance to the uncertainty in the total column of either ozone or water vapor is also treated analytically within SMAC. In the following, we applied the equation for gas X, with X being O~3~ or H~2~O.

The uncertainty in the total column $U_{X}$ is propagated through the Jacobian$\ J_{R_{toc}}^{U_{X}}$.

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                                                     |
+:===================================================================================================:+
| $$\mathrm{\Delta}_{R_{toc}}^{U_{X}} = \left| J_{R_{toc}}^{U_{X}}.\mathrm{\Delta}U_{X} \right|$$ (8) |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Within SMAC, it can also be derived analytically.

Let us recall the formulation of the transmission of the gas $T_{X}$:

|                                                   |
|:-------------------------------------------------:|
| $${T_{X} = e}^{{{\ a_{X}(U}_{X}m)}^{n_{X}}}$$ (9) |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

where $a_{X}$ and $n_{X}$ are SMAC coefficients and m is the air mass:

|                                                                  |
|:----------------------------------------------------------------:|
| $$m = \frac{1}{\cos\theta_{s}} + \frac{1}{\cos\theta_{v}}$$ (10) |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

We decompose Jacobian into two parts:

+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                                                              |
+:============================================================================================================:+
| $$J_{R_{toc}}^{U_{X}} = \frac{\partial R_{toc}}{\partial T_{X}}.\frac{\partial T_{X}}{\partial U_{X}}$$ (11) |
+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

------------------------------------------------------------------------

The sensitivity of $T_{X}$ to $U_{X}$ is:

+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                                                                        |
+:======================================================================================================================:+
| $$\frac{\partial T_{X}}{\partial U_{X}} = {\left( \frac{a_{X}.n_{X}}{U_{X}} \right).{\ (U}_{X}m)}^{n_{X}}.T_{X}$$ (12) |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

------------------------------------------------------------------------

The sensitivity of TOC directional reflectance to $T_{X}$ is obtained through Eqs. and using the decomposition${\ T}_{g} = T_{X}{\ .T}_{noX}$:

+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                                       |
+:=====================================================================================:+
| $$\frac{\partial R_{toc}}{\partial T_{X}} = {- \eta}^{2}\frac{TR_{toa}}{T_{X}}$$ (13) |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Surface Pressure and AOT at 550 nm

For the surface pressure ${\ P}_{s}$ and the AOT at 550 nm${\ \tau}_{a}^{550}$, we process the pixel two times more with small perturbations $\delta P_{s}$ and$\ \delta\tau_{a}^{550}$, and then derive the Jacobians using the finite differences:

+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                                                                                      |
+:====================================================================================================================================:+
| $$J_{R_{toc}}^{P_{s}} = \frac{{R_{toc}\left( P_{s} \right) - \rho}_{toc}\left( P_{s} - \delta P_{s} \right)}{\delta P_{s}}\ ,$$ (14) |
+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

------------------------------------------------------------------------

+-------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------+
|       | $\mathrm{\Delta}_{R_{toc}}^{P_{s}} = \left| J_{R_{toc}}^{P_{s}}.\mathrm{\Delta}P_{s} \right|$,                                                                         | \(15\) |
+=======+:=======================================================================================================================================================================+:=======+
|       | $$J_{R_{toc}}^{\tau_{a}^{550}} = \frac{{R_{toc}\left( \tau_{a}^{550} \right) - R}_{toc}\left( \tau_{a}^{550} - \delta\tau_{a}^{550} \right)}{\delta\tau_{a}^{550}}\ $$ | \(16\) |
+-------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------+
|       | $$\mathrm{\Delta}_{R_{toc}}^{\tau_{a}^{550}} = \left| J_{R_{toc}}^{\tau_{a}^{550}}.\mathrm{\Delta}\tau_{a}^{550} \right|$$                                             | \(17\) |
+-------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------+

We use$\ \delta P_{s} = 10\ hPa,\ \delta\tau_{a}^{550} = 0.1\ \tau_{a}^{550}$. These values are chosen to provoke a sufficient response in the AC scheme and still stay in the linear regime

# Validation Strategy

TOC-r's obtained with several aerosol ancillary data are validated with in-situ reference product obtained from AERONET data (version 3 of inverted AOD, spectral index of refraction and volume particle size distribution) and the 6S accurate atmospheric correction method. The comparison is made for N=48 AERONET sites (see Table 1) that cover different biomes, locations and meteorological conditions. Extraction of Proba-V data TOA-r and TOC-r' are done for 300 m products and TOA-r are processed with the 6S atmospheric correction software with AERONET data as inputs as it is done in the ACIX project (see Figure 1). TOC-r are evaluated using the same metrics as in ACIX

$A = \frac{1}{N}\left( \sum_{i = 1}^{N}{\Delta{toc\_ r}_{i}^{}} \right)$, Accuracy

$P = \sqrt{\frac{1}{N - 1}\left( \sum_{i = 1}^{N}\left( \Delta{toc\_ r}_{i}^{} - A \right)^{2} \right)}$, Precision

$U = \sqrt{\frac{1}{N_{}}\sum_{i = 1}^{N_{}}\left( \Delta{toc\_ r}_{i}^{} \right)^{2}}$, Uncertainty

with$\ \Delta{toc\_ r}_{i}^{} = \left( {toc\_ r}_{i}^{processor} - \ {toc\_ r}_{i}^{Aeronet} \right)$,

![](./media/media/image1.png){width="5.409448818897638in" height="2.952755905511811in"}

Figure : Validation methodology logic

::: {.content-visible when-format="docx"}
![](./media/media/image2.emf){width="5.8125in" height="3.1775317147856517in"}
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}
*(Figure omitted in PDF export: original diagram is a Windows EMF vector graphic not convertible in this environment; see the DOCX version of this ATBD for the figure.)*
:::

Table : List of AERONET sites used for validation

# Uncertainty Analysis Following FIDUCEO Approach

## Primary Measurement Equation

The TOC directional reflectance for each spectral band j is obtained through the SMAC atmospheric correction:

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
+:===================================================================================================================================================================================================:+
| $$R_{toc}\left( \theta_{s},\theta_{v},\Delta\Phi \right) = f\left( R_{toa},T_{g},T_{sca},R_{atm},s_{atm};U_{H_{2}O},U_{O_{3}},P_{s},\tau_{a}^{550},aerosol\_ model \right) + \varepsilon_{0}$$ (18) |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Where:

\- **R~toc~**: Top-of-Canopy directional reflectance (output)

\- **R~toa~**: Top-of-Atmosphere reflectance (primary input)

\- **T~g~**: Total gaseous transmission

\- **T~sca~**: Atmospheric scattering transmission

\- **R~atm~**: Atmospheric path reflectance

\- **s~atm~**: Atmospheric spherical albedo

\- **U~H₂O~**: Total column water vapor (auxiliary input from MERRA-2)

\- **U~O₃~**: Total column ozone (auxiliary input from MERRA-2)

\- **P~s~**: Surface pressure (derived from DEM and MERRA-2 SLP)

\- **τ~a~⁵⁵⁰**: Aerosol optical thickness at 550 nm (auxiliary input from MERRA-2)

\- **aerosol model**: Selected aerosol optical properties

\- **ε₀**: Second-order uncertainty terms

## Primary Uncertainty Equation

The total TOC reflectance uncertainty is derived from the measurement equation through uncertainty propagation:

+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
$$
u\left( R_{toc}^{j} \right) = \sqrt{
\left( \frac{\partial R_{toc}^{j}}{\partial R_{toa}^{j}}u(R_{toa}^{j}) \right)^{2}
+ \left( \frac{\partial R_{toc}^{j}}{\partial U_{H_2O}}u(U_{H_2O}) \right)^{2}
+ \left( \frac{\partial R_{toc}^{j}}{\partial U_{O_3}}u(U_{O_3}) \right)^{2}
+ \left( \frac{\partial R_{toc}^{j}}{\partial P_{S}}u(P_{S}) \right)^{2}
+ \left( \frac{\partial R_{toc}^{j}}{\partial \tau_{a}^{550}}u\left( \tau_{a}^{550} \right) \right)^{2}
+ \left( u\left( R_{toc}^{j} \right)_{ensemble} \right)^{2}
+ \left( u\left( R_{toc}^{j} \right)_{RTM} \right)^{2}
} + u^{2}(0)
\qquad (19)
$$

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Where individual contributions include:

- **TOA reflectance uncertainty** (from Level 1C, propagated with analytical Jacobians)
- **Gaseous absorption uncertainties** (O₃, H₂O, propagated with analytical Jacobians)
- **Surface pressure uncertainty** (from DEM + geolocation, propagated with finite difference)
- **Aerosol optical depth uncertainty** (from MERRA-2 validation studies, propagated with finite difference)
- **Aerosol model uncertainty** (from AMIP ensemble spread)
- **Radiative transfer approximation errors** (SMAC vs. exact RT, terrain slope, adjacency...)
- Second-order effects (u²(0) term)

## Effects Tables (FIDUCEO Methodology)

Following Popp and Mittaz (2022), each uncertainty source is characterized through "effects tables" documenting:

- Affected term in measurement equation

- Maturity of uncertainty estimate (0=none to 3=rigorous)

- Correlation structure (type and scale, spatial and temporal)

- PDF shape and magnitude

- Sensitivity coefficients

### Effect 1: TOA Reflectance Uncertainty

+------------------------------+----------------------------------+----------------------------------------------+
| Descriptor                   | Value                            | Notes                                        |
+:=============================+:=================================+:=============================================+
| Name                         | Direct TOA reflectance inversion |                                              |
+------------------------------+----------------------------------+----------------------------------------------+
| Affected term                | R~toc~ = f(R~toa~)               |                                              |
+------------------------------+----------------------------------+----------------------------------------------+
| Maturity                     | 3 - Rigorous analysis            | Direct propagation with analytical Jacobians |
+------------------------------+----------------------------------+----------------------------------------------+
| Correlation type             | From Level 1C product            | Random, structured, common components        |
+------------------------------+----------------------------------+----------------------------------------------+
| Correlation scale (spatial)  | Pixel-dependent                  | Structured: scan-line or column correlations |
+------------------------------+----------------------------------+----------------------------------------------+
| Correlation scale (temporal) | Scene-independent                | Random component uncorrelated                |
+------------------------------+----------------------------------+----------------------------------------------+
| PDF shape                    | Gaussian                         | Assumed from calibration analysis            |
+------------------------------+----------------------------------+----------------------------------------------+
| Magnitude                    | DTOA-r from L1C                  | Band-dependent, typically 0.01-0.03          |
+------------------------------+----------------------------------+----------------------------------------------+
| Sensitivity                  | c₁ = ∂R~toc~/∂R~toa~ = η²·T      | Analytical Jacobian from SMAC                |
+------------------------------+----------------------------------+----------------------------------------------+

Table TOA reflectance uncertainty summary

### Effect 2: Ozone Column Uncertainty

+------------------------------+-----------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------+
| Descriptor                   | Value                                   | Notes                                                       |
+:=============================+:========================================+:============================================================+
| Name                         | Total column ozone uncertainty          |                                                             |
+------------------------------+-----------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------+
| Affected term                | R~toc~ = f(U~O₃~) via T~g~              |                                                             |
+------------------------------+-----------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------+
| Maturity                     | 3 - Based on literature                 | MERRA-2 validation studies                                  |
+------------------------------+-----------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------+
| Correlation type             | Structured (regional synoptic patterns) |                                                             |
+------------------------------+-----------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------+
| Correlation scale (spatial)  | \~500 km                                | Synoptic weather systems                                    |
+------------------------------+-----------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------+
| Correlation scale (temporal) | \~3 days                                | Weather pattern persistence                                 |
+------------------------------+-----------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------+
| PDF shape                    | Gaussian                                |                                                             |
+------------------------------+-----------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------+
| Magnitude                    | 6% of UO₃ value                         | From Wargan et al. (2017), Davis et al. (2017)              |
+------------------------------+-----------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------+
| Sensitivity                  | c₂ = ∂R~toc~/∂U~O₃~                     | Analytical: ∂R~toc~/∂U~O₃~ = ∂R~toc~/∂T~O₃~ · ∂T~O₃~/∂U~O₃~ |
+------------------------------+-----------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------+

Table : Ozone uncertainty summary

### Effect 3: Water Vapor Column Uncertainty

+------------------------------+--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------+
| Descriptor                   | Value                                | Notes                                      |
+:=============================+:=====================================+:===========================================+
| Name                         | Total column water vapor uncertainty |                                            |
+------------------------------+--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------+
| Affected term                | R~toc~ = f(U~H₂O~) via T~g~          |                                            |
+------------------------------+--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------+
| Maturity                     | 2 - Estimated from literature        | MERRA-2 assessment                         |
+------------------------------+--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------+
| Correlation type             | Structured (weather fronts)          |                                            |
+------------------------------+--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------+
| Correlation scale (spatial)  | \~200 km                             | Convective systems                         |
+------------------------------+--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------+
| Correlation scale (temporal) | \~1 day                              | Rapid atmospheric changes                  |
+------------------------------+--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------+
| PDF shape                    | Gaussian                             |                                            |
+------------------------------+--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------+
| Magnitude                    | 20% of U~H₂O~ value                  | Conservative estimate (Davis et al., 2017) |
+------------------------------+--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------+
| Sensitivity                  | c₃ = ∂R~toc~/∂U~H₂O~                 | Analytical (same form as ozone)            |
+------------------------------+--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------+

Table : Water vapor uncertainty summary

### Effect 4: Surface Pressure Uncertainty {#effect-4-surface-pressure-uncertainty-1}

The uncertainty on the surface pressure $P_{S}$ is due to the uncertainty on the meteorological sea level pressure P~0~ from MERRA-2 SLP parameter (very low, \<1 hPa), and the way the extrapolation to the altitude of the surface z is done. z is obtained through GTOPO30 elev, which has a resolution close to the targeted satellite product. The uncertainty in the mean altitude of the pixel is ∆*z*.

The surface pressure *P~s~* is derived as:

+----------+--------------------------------------------------+----------+
|          | $$P_{S} = P_{0}\ e^{- \frac{g}{r\lambda}T^{'}}$$ | \(20\)   |
+==========+:=================================================+:=========+
|          | $$T^{'} = \ln(rT) - \ln(rT - r\lambda z)$$       | \(21\)   |
+----------+--------------------------------------------------+----------+

*Where T is the temperature above ground (from MERRA-2 T10M parameter), g* is Earth's gravity (9.80665 m.s^−2^), *r* is the gas constant for dry air (287.058 J.kg^−1^.K^−1^) and λ is the lapse rate *of the atmosphere, here taken* as −0.006 K.m^−1^ (*Van Besselaar et al., 2011*). The accuracy of this model depends on the variability of λ (typically ∆λ = 0.002 K.m^−1^). An uncertainty of $\mathrm{\Delta}_{P_{s}}^{\lambda}$\~1hPa could be assigned to this modeling error.

However, the uncertainty in the geolocation could result in a significant uncertainty ∆z, especially in rough terrain. The latter error could be partly a random error and a pixel dependent bias.

For example, if the geolocation error is purely random and isotropic, with a stable standard deviation, one may pre-calculate the standard deviation of the surface altitude as shown in Figure 2.

+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
+:
| ![](./media/media/image3.png){width="2.893700787401575in" height="2.425196850393701in"} ![](./media/media/image4.png){width="2.8976377952755907in" height="2.4606299212598426in"} |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Figure 2: Surface elevation (left) and local standard deviation of altitude (right), both in m, from GTOPO30.

We implemented the computation of the local standard deviation of the surface altitude as an experimental auxiliary dataset within the DEM NetCDF file. The dataset named Delev has been computed from the convolution of elev with a gaussian averaging kernel with a parameter $\sigma$ = 0.5 (in GTOPO30 bin unit, e.g 30 arc second). This correpsonds to the geometric accuracy of SPOT/VGT (0.3 pixel)

Finally, the uncertainty on P~s~ is:

+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                                                                                                               |
+:=============================================================================================================================================================:+
| $$u(P_{s}) = \sqrt{\left\lbrack \left( \mathrm{\Delta}_{P_{s}}^{\lambda} \right)^{2} + \left( \mathrm{\Delta}_{P_{s}}^{z} \right)^{2} \right\rbrack/2}$$ (22) |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

------------------------------------------------------------------------

with

+--------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                          |
+:========================================================================:+
| $$\mathrm{\Delta}_{P_{s}}^{z} = \left| J_{P_{s}}^{z}.u(z) \right|$$ (23) |
+--------------------------------------------------------------------------+

------------------------------------------------------------------------

and

|                                                            |
|:----------------------------------------------------------:|
| $$J_{P_{s}}^{z} = - \frac{gP_{S}}{r(T - \lambda z)}$$ (24) |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

+------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Descriptor                   | Value                                                                                    | Notes                                                                                |
+:=============================+:=========================================================================================+:=====================================================================================+
| Name                         | Surface pressure uncertainty                                                             |                                                                                      |
+------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Affected term                | Rtoc = f(Ps) via Rayleigh scattering                                                     |                                                                                      |
+------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Maturity                     | 3 - Rigorous propagation                                                                 | From DEM + meteorological uncertainty                                                |
+------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Correlation type             | Mixed: meteorological (common) + elevation (structured)                                  |                                                                                      |
+------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Correlation scale (spatial)  | Meteo: global; Elev: DEM resolution                                                      |                                                                                      |
+------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Correlation scale (temporal) | Meteo: \>1 month; Elev: permanent                                                        |                                                                                      |
+------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| PDF shape                    | Gaussian                                                                                 |                                                                                      |
+------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Magnitude                    | u(Ps) = √\[($\mathrm{\Delta}_{P_{s}}^{\lambda}$)² + ($\mathrm{\Delta}_{P_{s}}^{z}$)²\]/2 | $\mathrm{\Delta}_{P_{s}}^{\lambda}$ \~1 hPa, $\mathrm{\Delta}_{P_{s}}^{z}$from Delev |
+------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Sensitivity                  | c₄ = ∂Rtoc/∂Ps                                                                           | Finite difference: δPs = 10 hPa                                                      |
+------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

Table : Surface pressure uncertainty summary

> **Critical Implementation Note (v1.1):** the computation of $T^{'} = \ln(rT) - \ln(rT - r\lambda z)$ overflows in float16 for typical temperatures ($T \approx 290$ K, $rT \approx 83246$, which exceeds the float16 maximum of 65504), producing NaN pressure and NaN Rtoc. The implementation therefore stores and processes `SLP` and `T10M` as **float32** in the ancillary cache (`_MERRA_CACHE_FLOAT32_VARS = {'SLP', 'T10M'}`). See Appendix D.2 for details.

### Effect 5: Aerosol Optical Depth Uncertainty

Quantifying aerosol optical depth (AOD) uncertainty in global chemical transport models (CTMs) remains critical for climate and air quality applications. Recent studies have highlighted systematic biases and uncertainties in leading reanalysis products such as CAMS and MERRA-2.

Comparative validation studies against AERONET ground-based observations reveal that both CAMS and MERRA-2 exhibit regional biases, with typical AOD uncertainties ranging from 30-50% depending on aerosol type and geographic location. Model-observation discrepancies are particularly pronounced over dust-dominated regions and biomass burning areas, where AOD can be underestimated by factors of 1.5-2.0.

Advances in uncertainty quantification have emerged through ensemble approaches and data assimilation techniques. Studies show that assimilating satellite retrievals can reduce AOD uncertainty by 20-35%, though systematic errors in aerosol vertical distribution persist. Emission inventory uncertainties, particularly for anthropogenic sources in rapidly developing regions, can account for 25-40% of total AOD uncertainty.

The community is moving toward probabilistic frameworks that explicitly quantify measurement uncertainties, model uncertainties, and structural uncertainties arising from parameterization choices in aerosol microphysics, chemistry, and removal processes.

The AOD uncertainties (Root Mean Square Errors) could be assigned on a regional scale using a climate type classification map like the one used in Gueymard and Yang 2020. Years of AOD \@550 and Angström exponent 3-hourly datasets from MERRA-2 and CAMS have been evaluated at 793 AERONET stations. The validation was conducted based on continents and Köppen-Geiger classification (see Figure 3).

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                                                                                                           |
+:==========================================================================================================================================================+
| ![](./media/media/image5.png){width="5.479861111111111in" height="3.6in"} |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Figure : Map of Köppen-Geiger climate classification

#### RMSE Model

Based on the Gueymard and Yang findings, a practical operational uncertainty predictor can be formulated as:

$$\sigma\left( \tau_{a}^{550} \right) = \max\left( \sigma_{base},\sigma_{relative} \times \tau_{a}^{550} \right)$$

Where:

\- σ~base~ = 0.03 (clean atmosphere baseline)

\- σ~relative~ = 0.15 (15% relative uncertainty)

This is justified by the following arguments

\- Low AOD regime (τ~a~ \< 0.2): Dominated by instrument noise \~0.03

\- Moderate-high AOD (τ~a~ ≥ 0.2): Scales with aerosol loading → 15% uncertainty

We intend in an **updated version** to apply Köppen-Geiger climate classification multipliers:

+----------------------+----------------+----------------------------+
| Region Type          | Multiplier     | Example Locations          |
+:=====================+:===============+:===========================+
| Oceanic/Polar        | 0.7×           | Open oceans, Arctic        |
+----------------------+----------------+----------------------------+
| North America/Europe | 1.0×           | Baseline reference         |
+----------------------+----------------+----------------------------+
| Dust-dominated       | 1.5×           | Sahara, Arabian Peninsula  |
+----------------------+----------------+----------------------------+
| Biomass burning      | 1.4×           | Amazon, Sub-Saharan Africa |
+----------------------+----------------+----------------------------+
| High pollution       | 1.3×           | East Asia, South Asia      |
+----------------------+----------------+----------------------------+

+------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------+
| Descriptor                   | Value                                                                                | Notes                               |
+:=============================+:=====================================================================================+:====================================+
| Name                         | AOD at 550 nm uncertainty                                                            |                                     |
+------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------+
| Affected term                | Rtoc = f(τ~a~⁵⁵⁰)                                                                    |                                     |
+------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------+
| Maturity                     | 3 - Based on comprehensive validation                                                | Gueymard & Yang (2020) study        |
+------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------+
| Correlation type             | Structured (aerosol transport patterns)                                              |                                     |
+------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------+
| Correlation scale (spatial)  | Regional (\~200-500 km)                                                              | Aerosol plume extent                |
+------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------+
| Correlation scale (temporal) | \~1 week                                                                             | Aerosol lifetime                    |
+------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------+
| PDF shape                    | Skewed Gaussian (log-normal for high AOD)                                            |                                     |
+------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------+
| Magnitude                    | σ(τ~a~⁵⁵⁰) = max(0.03, 0.15×τ~a~⁵⁵⁰), and optionally regional multipliers (see text) | Based on Gueymard & Yang validation |
+------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------+

Table AOD550 uncertainty summary

### Effect 6: Aerosol Model Selection Uncertainty

The SMAC coefficients are computed by an in-house radiative transfer model depending on the aerosol mixture (see section 9)

- X~u~ = $\frac{\tau_{a;u}^{550}}{\tau_{a}^{550}}$ \[u $\in \{ DU,BC,OC,SE,SU\}\rbrack$ from MERRA-2

- iaero = $\text{argmin}_i \sum_{u} \left( X_u - X_{u,i} \right)^{2}$, for $i=0,\ldots,147$ (currently one model selected)

- The X~u,i~ basis for the 148 predefined models are stored in a text file and read once.

So, it is possible simply to choose a couple of aerosol models that fit best with the MERRA-2 data and run SMAC-CL on an ensemble of models. We rely here on the AMIP dataset (see section 2.4) that yields an ensemble of 10 aerosols models globally monthly. An example is given in Figure 4 .

+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
+:============================================================================================================================================================================================================+
| ![](./media/media/image6.png){width="5.0078740157480315in" height="7.437007874015748in"} |
+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Figure : SMAC Aerosol model index (0-147) reconstructed from the AMIP ensemble runs #1-10 for December 2010

+------------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------+------------------------+
| Descriptor                   | Value                                                               | Notes                  |
+:=============================+:====================================================================+:=======================+
| Name                         | Aerosol optical properties (model type)                             |                        |
+------------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------+------------------------+
| Affected term                | Rtoc = f(aerosol_model)                                             |                        |
+------------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------+------------------------+
| Maturity                     | 2 - Ensemble approach                                               | AMIP 10-member spread  |
+------------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------+------------------------+
| Correlation type             | Structured (regional aerosol regimes)                               |                        |
+------------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------+------------------------+
| Correlation scale (spatial)  | \~100 km (1° grid)                                                  | Climatology resolution |
+------------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------+------------------------+
| Correlation scale (temporal) | \~1 week                                                            | Aerosol type changes   |
+------------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------+------------------------+
| PDF shape                    | Empirical (ensemble spread)                                         |                        |
+------------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------+------------------------+
| Magnitude                    | u_Rtoc_ensemble = std(Rtoc_j), j=1..10                              | From AMIP runs         |
+------------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------+------------------------+
| Sensitivity                  | Implicit in ensemble                                                | No explicit Jacobian   |
+------------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------+------------------------+
|                              | `For each pixel:`\                                                  |                        |
|                              | `1. Select 10 preferred aerosol models from 148 options`\           |                        |
|                              | `(based on MERRA-2 species fractions matching AMIP ensemble)`\      |                        |
|                              | `2. Run SMAC with each model → Rtoc,1 ... Rtoc,10`\                 |                        |
|                              | ``` 3. ``u_Rtoc_ensemble`` = ``standard_deviation``(``Rtoc,j``) ``` |                        |
+------------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------+------------------------+

Table : Aerosol model uncertainty summary

### Effect 7: Radiative Transfer Approximations

Three sources of RT approximation errors:

#### 7a. Lambertian Surface Assumption

It is most important for NIR and strong aerosol loading (in that case uncertainty due to AOD dominating). The modelling of the effect and a way to estimate the uncertainty associated is described in section 8.5. The surface BRDF shape is modeled using two parameters ${\ k}_{1}^{p}$ and $k_{2}^{p}$, depending on spectral band.

In the baseline run, the surface is supposed to be lambertian (in that case${\ k}_{1}^{p} = k_{2}^{p} = 0$). In a second run, those two parameters are assigned a climatological value, for example coming from the C3S Multi Sensor Surface Albedo products k~0~, k~1~ and k~2~ with $k_{1}^{p} = \ \frac{k_{1}}{k_{0}}\ ;\ k_{2}^{p} = \ \frac{k_{2}}{k_{0}}$

Difference in R~toc~ from the two runs gives an estimation of the BRDF effects.

$u\left( R_{toc}^{j} \right)_{RTM\_BRDF} = \left( {\ k}_{1}^{p}=0,k_{2}^{p} =0 \right) - \left( {\ k}_{1}^{p} \neq 0, k_{2}^{p} \neq 0 \right)$
+--------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------+
| Descriptor   | Value                                                                                                                                                                | Notes                               |
+:=============+:=====================================================================================================================================================================+:====================================+
| Significance | Moderate (NIR, high AOD)                                                                                                                                             |                                     |
+--------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------+
| Maturity     | 1 - Initial assessment                                                                                                                                               | From 6S validation                  |
+--------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------+
| Magnitude    | \~1% for most cases                                                                                                                                                  | Up to 5% for strong BRDF + high AOD |
+--------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------+
| Mitigation   | Using BRDF shape included in the product (https://dast.copernicus-climate.eu/documents/satellite-albedo/D1.3.4-v2.0_ATBD_CDR_SA_MULTI_SENSOR_v2.0_PRODUCTS_v1.1.pdf) |                                     |
+--------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------+

Table : RTM Lambertian assumption uncertainty summary

#### 7b. Terrain Slope Effects
